software_it.label: Software & IT
software_it.explainer: When AI coding assistants and agent frameworks surge, it signals that the tools developers use daily are becoming more autonomous. Research from Stanford and ADP shows that entry-level software developer roles contracted between late 2022 and mid-2025, while demand for senior developers remained stable or grew. These tools amplify experienced developers while reducing demand for junior ones.

legal.label: Legal & Compliance
legal.explainer: These tools convert stacks of documents into structured data that AI can analyze. When adoption accelerates, it means companies are building systems to automate document review, contract analysis, regulatory compliance checks, and due diligence — tasks currently done by paralegals, junior associates, and compliance staff. Harvey AI (a legal AI startup) has raised $300M+, signaling major venture capital conviction that these workflows will be automated.

finance.label: Finance & Insurance
finance.explainer: Financial services runs on documents — loan applications, insurance claims, regulatory filings, tax returns. When AI document processing tools accelerate, it signals automation of the workflows that employ millions of back-office workers in banking, insurance, and accounting. J.P. Morgan research found a mildly negative correlation between AI usage and employment trends in financial services subsectors.

healthcare.label: Healthcare
healthcare.explainer: Healthcare AI tools are growing slowly compared to other industries. This makes sense — healthcare has high regulatory barriers (HIPAA, FDA), requires clinical validation, and stakes are literally life and death. The slow signal here suggests healthcare workers face less near-term automation pressure from open-source AI tools. However, proprietary tools (like ambient clinical documentation from Abridge or Nuance/DAX) are growing fast — they just don't show up in this open-source data.

creative.label: Creative & Media
creative.explainer: AI image generation had explosive growth in 2022-2023 but has recently stabilized (diffusers actually declined recently). Meanwhile, speech-to-text tools continue growing steadily. The creative industry signal is nuanced: the initial wave of AI image generation disrupted stock photography and simple illustration, but more complex creative work has been harder to automate. The stabilization in image generation tools may reflect market maturation rather than declining impact.

office.label: Office & Administrative
office.explainer: These are the broadest signals — general-purpose AI agents and automation tools that can be applied to any office workflow. When browser automation (browser-use) or multi-agent frameworks (crewai) surge, it signals that developers are building tools to replace repetitive tasks across all administrative functions. This is the category most aligned with the "AI is coming for white-collar work" narrative, and the one where employment data is most worth watching.
